reason: Both give the same correct verdict: skip full event sourcing, use an append-only ledger plus boring CRUD, add idempotent usage ingestion, consider Stripe/Metronome/Lago, and confine any ES experiment to a narrow bounded context. Both correctly cite event versioning and eventual-consistency UX as the core reasons ES is wrong here. A adds a few extra specifics (double-entry ledger framing, upcasters/snapshotting, storing computed amounts in events) but these elaborate on points B already makes rather than introducing new decision-relevant facts. B is more concise and equally actionable. No clear quality gap between them.
